title: Brief Overview of Remote
pros:
All remote workers are 100% remote. In an admin role, you will never be asked to come into the office. There is no geographical requirement from what I understand, the only requirement (aside from having satisfactory experience) is a good internet connection. 

The hours are strict which is good and bad. If you are hired for an 8:30-5PM shift....you will work those hours. Aside from requested flexibility, you can ensure that you will start and end at the times you were hired for.
cons:
There is occasional reorganization in the departments and each person job duties. 

Not many holidays off.

title: Rewarding Place To Work
pros:
Opportunities for growth
Offers benefits
Flexible schedules with consistent hours
Great work environment
Vacation/Sick time
Yearly performance reviews
Remote work options

From my experience working here, as long as you show you're committed and have work ethic, there's always room for growth.  It's possible to start low as an office admin up to management.
cons: I don't have much to complain about, sometimes management can be direct, and expect prompt responses, but that's due to the fast paced environment.
advice_to_management: ABCO Facility Maintenance is a great place to work. I greatly appreciate the opportunities for growth offered here with the chance to learn new things. Your hard work feels appreciated.

title: Avoid this business. Being homeless would be better. 0 Stars
pros: None at all. Worst place of work ever
cons: Owners are rude and lie. They micro manage and the work environment is hostile. Don’t work here unless you enjoy misery. Once you sign on they believe they own you 24x7. The company is run like a 3rd world dictatorship. The tech is so old and management has zero interest in updating it. 0 stars
advice_to_management: Allow people to do their jobs without enforcing fantasy world deadlines. Treat people with a minuscule of respect.

title: Remote Flexibility Overshadowed by Poor Management and Unstable Practices
pros:
Fully remote work allows flexibility and eliminates commuting costs.

A chance to work independently and develop problem-solving skills.
cons:
-Lack of transparency regarding benefits, including vacation time.
-Poor management support; requests for guidance, SOPs, or feedback often go unanswered.
-Job security is unstable; employees can be let go without valid reason or warning.
-Limited opportunities for professional growth or advancement.
-Evasive communication and unprofessional handling of employee concerns.
-Unfair practices in handling earned benefits after layoffs.
-A culture that prioritizes profit over employee well-being and support.
advice_to_management:
-Be transparent and consistent about employee benefits and ensure employees receive what they’ve earned.
-Provide proper training, guidance, and resources to help employees succeed in their roles.
-Implement clear SOPs and policies to reduce confusion and frustration.
-Create a supportive environment that values employee contributions and fosters growth.
-Develop a structured promotion and review process to retain top talent and boost morale.
-Treat employees as valuable assets, not as disposable resources.

title: ziprecruiter
pros: I am sure you are a great company. Your Talent Acquisition Team need to take a deep dive into your application process as it is tedious and not user-friendly. I can help you change that. The employee lifecycle begins at the attraction/application stage.
cons: You are losing out on good people who want to work for your organization but can't get past your ATS system. Not a good start to the employee experience.
